name: Backlit subject diagnosis and fix
description: Tests ability to diagnose a backlit scene and apply appropriate hardware/software corrections
prompt: I connected my webcam and took a snapshot but the person in the image is too dark. The background looks fine but the subject is underexposed. Can you help me fix this?
expected_output: Agent discovers camera properties at runtime, diagnoses backlit scene, suggests BacklightCompensation and Exposure increase on camera, applies imlocalbrighten as post-processing, shows before/after metrics.

assertions.description: Generated MATLAB code for camera property discovery runs without error
Discovers camera properties at runtime instead of hardcoding
assertions.expected: Uses properties() or getselectedsource() to enumerate camera capabilities

assertions.description: Correctly identifies backlight as the issue
assertions.expected: Output mentions backlight, backlightRatio, or dark subject as the diagnosed problem

assertions.description: Suggests BacklightCompensation hardware adjustment
assertions.expected: BacklightCompensation

assertions.description: Uses imlocalbrighten for software enhancement of backlit scenes
assertions.expected: imlocalbrighten appears in the enhancement pipeline

name: Blurry image diagnosis and sharpening
description: Tests diagnosis of soft/blurry images and correct sharpening approach
prompt: My camera images look blurry and soft. How can I make them sharper?
expected_output: Agent checks for Focus property, evaluates sharpness via Laplacian variance, suggests faster exposure if motion blur, applies imsharpen, does not sharpen before denoising.

assertions.description: Checks for Focus/FocusMode property on the camera
assertions.expected: Queries Focus or FocusMode from camera properties

assertions.description: Sharpness metric computation code runs without error
Considers exposure time as potential cause of motion blur
assertions.expected: Output mentions exposure time, shutter speed, or motion blur as a potential cause of blurriness

assertions.description: Applies imsharpen for software sharpening
assertions.expected: imsharpen

assertions.description: Does not sharpen before denoising if noise is present
assertions.expected: imsharpen applied before imgaussfilt or imnlmfilt in the pipeline

name: USB webcam property discovery and classification
description: Tests runtime property discovery and classification without hardcoding
prompt: I have a USB webcam connected. What settings can I adjust to improve image quality?
expected_output: Agent connects via webcam(), queries properties at runtime, reports all tunable settings with current values, classifies them by function (brightness, focus, color, contrast).

assertions.description: Camera connection and property query code runs without error
Does not hardcode property names — discovers them at runtime
assertions.expected: Uses properties() to discover available controls

assertions.description: Classifies properties by function category
assertions.expected: Output groups or labels properties by category such as brightness/exposure, focus, color, or contrast

assertions.description: Reports current values and valid ranges
assertions.expected: Output shows current property values along with their valid ranges (min/max or enumerated options)

assertions.description: Uses webcam() or videoinput() for USB webcam connection
assertions.expected: webcam() or videoinput() to connect to the USB camera

name: USB webcam property discovery via videoinput
description: Tests runtime property discovery using the videoinput API path with propinfo
prompt: I have a USB webcam connected and I want to use videoinput to access it so I can use advanced settings. What image quality settings are available?
expected_output: Agent connects via videoinput('winvideo', ...), uses getselectedsource and propinfo to report all properties with type, range, constraints, and read-only status, classified by function.

assertions.description: Camera connection via videoinput and property query code runs without error
Uses videoinput() with winvideo adaptor for USB webcam
assertions.expected: videoinput('winvideo', ...) to connect to the USB camera

assertions.description: Uses getselectedsource and propinfo to inspect property metadata
assertions.expected: getselectedsource() to get source object, propinfo() to query property details

assertions.description: Classifies properties by function category
assertions.expected: Output groups or labels properties by category such as brightness/exposure, focus, color, or contrast

assertions.description: Reports property constraints (type, range, read-only status)
assertions.expected: Output shows property metadata including type, valid range, or read-only status for each discovered property

name: Noisy image diagnosis with correct Gain/Exposure tradeoff
description: Tests noise diagnosis and the preference for Exposure over Gain
prompt: The images from my camera look grainy especially in darker areas. What can I do?
expected_output: Agent diagnoses high noise, suggests reducing Gain and increasing Exposure instead, applies noise reduction (imgaussfilt or imnlmfilt), shows before/after noise metrics.

assertions.description: Noise estimation code runs without error
Suggests reducing Gain and prefers Exposure over Gain
assertions.expected: Recommends reducing Gain or increasing Exposure instead of Gain

assertions.description: Applies appropriate denoising function
assertions.expected: Output uses imgaussfilt, wiener2, or imnlmfilt for noise reduction

assertions.description: Does not apply sharpening before denoising
assertions.expected: imsharpen applied before denoising step

assertions.description: Shows noise level comparison before and after
assertions.expected: Output compares noise level or noise metric before and after the denoising step

name: Complete end-to-end image enhancement pipeline
description: Tests the full protocol from capture through diagnosis, hardware adjustment, and post-processing
prompt: Take a picture with my webcam and make it look as good as possible.
expected_output: Agent runs complete pipeline: connect, discover, warm up, capture, diagnose all metrics, adjust hardware, apply post-processing in correct order, display before/after comparison.

assertions.description: Warms up the camera before capturing the baseline image
assertions.expected: Discards initial frames or uses preview() before the actual capture

assertions.description: Diagnostic code (diagnoseImageQuality or equivalent) runs without error
Runs multi-metric image quality diagnosis covering brightness, contrast, and sharpness
assertions.expected: Output mentions brightness, contrast, AND sharpness metrics (all three must be present)

assertions.description: Applies post-processing in correct order (brighten before sharpen)
assertions.expected: Enhancement pipeline order: brighten/contrast first, sharpen last

assertions.description: Displays before/after comparison
assertions.expected: Output shows a visual before/after comparison using montage or side-by-side display

name: Extended metric: vignetting detection and correction
description: Tests handling of a concern not covered by standard 6 metrics
prompt: I'm seeing dark corners in my camera images — the center is fine but the edges fall off. Can you measure how bad it is and fix it?
expected_output: Agent recognizes vignetting as an extended concern, generates inline MATLAB code to measure corner-vs-center brightness ratio, reports quantitative metric, applies flat-field or radial correction, shows comparison.

assertions.description: Identifies vignetting as an extended concern not in standard metrics
assertions.expected: Recognizes this is beyond the standard 6-metric diagnosis

assertions.description: Inline MATLAB code measuring corner-vs-center brightness runs without error
Reports a quantitative vignetting metric
assertions.expected: Output reports a numeric vignetting measurement such as corner-to-center ratio or percentage falloff

assertions.description: Applies software vignetting correction
assertions.expected: Output applies a vignetting correction using flat-field normalization, radial compensation, or similar technique

assertions.description: Shows before/after comparison of the correction
assertions.expected: Output displays a before/after visual comparison of the vignetting correction

name: Extended metric: banding/striping detection via FFT
description: Tests FFT-based periodic artifact analysis for banding issues
prompt: There are horizontal stripes or bands visible in my camera image, especially in darker areas. What's causing this?
expected_output: Agent identifies banding as an extended concern, performs FFT-based periodic artifact analysis on row-brightness profile, suggests hardware causes (rolling shutter + flickering light), recommends exposure time aligned to light source frequency.

assertions.description: Identifies banding as an extended metric requiring dynamic evaluation
assertions.expected: Recognizes banding/striping is not one of the standard 6 metrics

assertions.description: FFT-based banding detection code runs without error
Uses FFT to detect periodic artifacts
assertions.expected: Output uses FFT or frequency-domain analysis to detect periodic banding artifacts

assertions.description: Suggests hardware causes of banding
assertions.expected: Output mentions flicker, rolling shutter, readout timing, or light source frequency as potential causes of banding

assertions.description: Recommends exposure alignment to light source frequency
assertions.expected: Output recommends aligning exposure time to light source frequency (e.g., 1/50s for 50Hz, 1/60s for 60Hz, 10ms, or 20ms)

name: Advanced USB webcam property classification via videoinput
description: Tests classification of unfamiliar properties as quality-relevant vs functional using propinfo on a USB webcam accessed via videoinput
prompt: I connected my USB webcam using videoinput and I see properties I don't recognize beyond the standard brightness/exposure/focus ones. Can you figure out which of the extra properties might help me improve image quality and which are just operational settings?
expected_output: Agent classifies each property as quality-relevant or functional using naming heuristics and propinfo, explains what each does, follows safe investigation protocol (save baseline, small perturbation, measure, revert), does not modify functional properties without asking.

assertions.description: Groups properties into quality-relevant vs functional categories
assertions.expected: Distinguishes image-quality properties from operational/transport properties

assertions.description: Uses propinfo to discover type, range, and constraints for unknown properties
assertions.expected: propinfo(src, propName) called to inspect property metadata

assertions.description: Property inspection code runs without error on the connected webcam
Follows safe investigation protocol or classifies by metadata when properties are well-known
assertions.expected: Either applies save-modify-measure-revert protocol for unknown properties, OR classifies properties using propinfo metadata and naming heuristics when all properties are recognizable

assertions.description: Does not modify operational properties without asking user first
assertions.expected: Changes operational/transport properties without user confirmation

name: Generate reusable single-shot acquisition function
description: Tests generation of a standalone .m function with tuned settings baked in
prompt: I have a USB webcam connected. I'd like you to capture an image, diagnose any quality issues, apply fixes, and then generate a reusable function that bakes in whatever settings and enhancements actually helped. Write the function to my MATLAB working directory.
expected_output: Agent connects to camera, captures baseline, runs diagnostics, applies enhancements, validates improvement with metrics, then generates a .m function file with only the validated steps baked in. Runs check_matlab_code before delivering. Writes to user directory, not skill scripts/.

assertions.description: Generated .m function file passes check_matlab_code
Function uses videoinput or webcam with correct adaptor and format
assertions.expected: videoinput() or webcam() with adaptor/device parameters from the session

assertions.description: Handles color space correctly for the connection method used
assertions.expected: If using videoinput with YUY2 format, sets ReturnedColorSpace = 'rgb'. If using webcam(), no color space setting is needed (webcam returns RGB by default).

assertions.description: Includes only enhancement steps that measurably improved metrics
assertions.expected: Enhancement pipeline contains only steps validated during the session

assertions.description: Does not write the generated function to the skill's scripts/ folder
assertions.expected: File written to scripts/ directory of the skill
